question_text: An output restricted Deque (ORDeque) is implemented using a circular array. Complete Enqueue_ORDeque(Q, x, a), Dequeue_ORDeque(Q), and IsFull(Q) for insertion at rear/front, deletion from rear, and full-queue detection.

items.description: Checks if the queue is full using isFull(Q), prints Queue Full, and returns before enqueueing.
For a = 0, inserts from rear by circularly updating rear: if rear = Q.size then rear = 1 else rear = rear + 1, then stores x in Q[rear].
For a = 1, inserts from front by setting Q[front] = x and circularly updating front: if front = 1 then front = Q.size else front = front - 1.
In Dequeue_ORDeque, checks if front = rear and reports that the queue is empty.
Extracts the element before updating the pointer by assigning x = Q[rear].
Updates rear circularly after dequeue: if rear = 1 then rear = Q.size else rear = rear - 1, then returns x.
In IsFull(Q), checks the condition front = rear + 1 and returns true when it holds.
In IsFull(Q), checks the condition front = 1 and rear = Q.size, otherwise returns false.
